# All Lectures Should Be Recorded and Posted to the Class Website: An Analytical Dive into Possibly Making a Great Class Even Better 

## What do people think about having a recorded lecture?

A survey indicates that more students than not would find it at least somewhat helpful if lecture was recorded and posted to the class website. A whopping 358 students in favor versus 176 against or with no opinion. 
<img src="/static/imgs/image-3.png" alt="Histogram of Lesson Video Ratings" width="500"/>

Furthermore, the number of individuals who think having some sort of pre-class video would be somewhat beneficial outweighs those opposed with a vote of 388 to 146. 
<img src="/static/imgs/image-1.png" alt="Histogram of Pre-Lesson Video Ratings" width="500"/>

In addition to both of these, when asked about a livestream, 393 students were not opposed, while only 141 were, or were neutral. The raw data indicates that the student body wants lecture, or at least some content from this course, to be recorded.
<img src="/static/imgs/image-2.png" alt="Histogram of Livestream Ratings" width="500"/>



## What do the specific population breakdowns look like?

The proportion of students who think that lecture recordings are helpful make up the majority of the population. However, sometimes it is beneficial to see a more visual representation of the population density.
<img src="/static/imgs/image-4.png" alt="Violin Plot of Lesson Video Ratings" width="500"/>
While this plot may help roughly vizualize the proportion of the population that wants lecture recorded, an empirical cumulative distribution plot can combine the numerical analysis of the histogram and maintain its ability to convey proportion size like the violin plot. 
<img src="/static/imgs/image-5.png" alt="Empirical Cumulative Distribution Plot of Lesson Video Ratings" width="500"/>
The same two analyses can be done when asked about pre-class videos and livestreams.
<img src="/static/imgs/image-6.png" alt="Violin Plot of Pre-Lecture Video Ratings" width="500"/>
<img src="/static/imgs/image-7.png" alt="Empirical Cumulative Distribution Plot of Pre-Lecture Video Ratings" width="500"/>
When asked about pre-recorded content, students felt similarly; they were in favor of it. 
<img src="/static/imgs/image-8.png" alt="Violin Plot of Livestream Ratings" width="500"/>
<img src="/static/imgs/image-9.png" alt="Empirical Cumulative Distribution Plot of Livestream Ratings" width="500"/>
The same can be said about the livestream idea.
